priority_directive.statement: Weight sentiment analysis toward approved PIRs: PIR-06 (PN-Bersatu removal/expulsion/withdrawal narratives), PIR-09 (candidate credibility — disciplinary actions, defections, eligibility), PIR-07 (battleground seats — campaign momentum, candidate reception).
priority_directive.critical_flag_directive: Flag any CRITICAL sentiment shift related to PIR-06 (PN-removal-of-Bersatu).
priority_directive.shift_threshold: Flag any entity with sharp sentiment shift (>30% change from previous cycle).

prior_revision.coverage: 86 source files / 264 entities; cumulative 49 entities scored (15 pos / 18 neu / 16 neg)
prior_revision.key_finding: PIR-06 ESCALATED from senior-figure advocacy (rev4: Kiandee) to TWO-SIDED/THREE-SIDED principal-level coalition-restructuring motion (rev5: Bersatu PRESIDENT Muhyiddin publicly hints at exit + signals new post-election bloc; Annuar Musa corroborates; PAS openly sidelines Bersatu; Hadi blame-shifts; mainstream 'imminent?' framing). NO formal notice of either type. CRITICAL flag raised (second consecutive revision to escalate).

this_revision.method_note: Sentiment based on verified source content only (CVS standard). The entities_190215.json now carries the PIR-06 principal actors the rev5 pass had to score via direct source attribution; the rev6 delta is therefore both a sentiment update AND a data-quality reconciliation. The Annuar Musa speaker-label ambiguity is carried as a CAVEAT (see data_gaps_rev6): the 'part-ways/own-logo' statement is retained but its speaker attribution to 'Bersatu sec-gen Annuar Musa' is unverified — the known Bersatu Sec-Gen is Hamzah Zainudin. This does not change the PIR-06 two-sided assessment (Muhyiddin's exit-hint is independently corroborated and is the principal signal), but the Annuar Musa entity score is retained with reduced confidence.

negative.entity: [Rev6Δ][PIR-09] Tamim Dahri (activist) — surrenders to police (Port Dickson, FB-threat case); intends to campaign for an independent NS candidate
negative.category: figure (activist / independent-candidate support network) — candidate-credibility / legal
negative.trend: NEW — DEVELOPMENT. Tamim Dahri (a PIR-09 entity, listed in entities_165723.json PIR-09 key_entities) surrendered to police in Port Dickson over a Facebook-threat case (cf. earlier Utusan 'Aktivis diburu polis serah diri di Port Dickson') and now states he hopes to campaign for an N Sembilan independent candidate. The independent candidate is not named in the captured text. This is a candidate-credibility/legal-development datum: an activist figure associated with the independent-candidate support network has a live legal matter, yet intends to campaign. NEW relative to rev5 (Tamim was listed as an entity but not sentiment-scored; the surrender-to-police development is freshly surfaced in the 194028 cycle).
negative.rationale: Malaysiakini (mkini, 194028 gnews + homepage): 'Tamim surrenders to police, hopes to campaign for N Sembilan independent candidate.' Negative for the independent-candidate credibility axis (PIR-09): a support-network figure with a live legal case publicly attaching to a campaign is a character/credibility signal. The independent candidate's identity is unconfirmed in the captured text — watch for the named candidate (Datuk A Saravanan N.33 background vetting is still pending; possible linkage). Negative but moderate (0.50): a legal matter + campaign intent, not a candidate disqualification or expulsion.
sources: utusancommy_20260718_194028.md ('Aktivis diburu polis serah diri di Port Dickson')
negative.source_reliability: HIGH — Malaysiakini (homepage + gnews); Utusan corroborating on the police-search antecedent. Self-reported surrender.

negative.entity: [Rev6Δ][PIR-06] Muhyiddin graft-trial surfacing — 'Company gave Bersatu RM1m for unknown reasons' + 'Muhyiddin terima RM24.4 juta bayaran projek Jana Wibawa' (corroborating legal/credibility datum on the PIR-06 principal)
negative.trend: NEW (surfacing). The Muhyiddin graft trial — previously peripheral — resurfaced in the 194028 gnews/WP-API feeds: (a) Malaysiakini 'Company gave Bersatu RM1m for unknown reasons, witness tells court in Muhyiddin's graft trial'; (b) Utusan 'Syarikat dicadangkan Muhyiddin terima RM24.4 juta bayaran projek Jana Wibawa'. These are '1 d ago' items that rotated back into the top-60 result sets. Not a same-day development, but a corroborating negative on the PIR-06 principal's credibility that now sits alongside his exit-hint statement.
negative.rationale: The graft-trial surfacing compounds the negative sentiment on Muhyiddin (PIR-06 principal): the same figure publicly hinting at Bersatu's exit and 'new bloc' formation is concurrently on trial for RM24.4m Jana Wibawa payments + a RM1m Bersatu receipt. This is a credibility backdrop, not a PIR-06 coalition-restructuring event — logged as a corroborating negative on the principal. It marginally reinforces the negative read on Muhyiddin (the principal signaling exit is also legally encumbered), but does NOT change the two-sided motion assessment. Scored as a distinct entity (the trial surfacing) rather than folded into the Muhyiddin exit-hint entity, because it is a separate legal proceeding.
negative.source_reliability: HIGH — Malaysiakini + Utusan court reporting (witness testimony on record).

This is the MCA-leadership response to (a) the BN-PN electoral understanding (rev5) and (b) the MCA silencing of Youth sec-gen Saw Yee Fung over her BN-PN criticism (rev5 PIR-09). It is a managed-defence statement, not a new escalation.

PIR-07 (battleground/coalition narrative) + PIR-09 adjacency (component discipline context). Watch: whether MCA rank-and-file accepts the 'not a merger' framing or further dissent emerges (cf. 'Pemimpin MCA persoal dakwaan kerjasama UMNO-Pas').

neutral.entity: [Rev6Δ][PIR-07] BN confidence in simple majority — 'BN yakin menang majoriti mudah PRN Negeri Sembilan, bentuk kerjaan tanpa parti lain' (mStar)
neutral.trend: NEW — BN CONFIDENCE SIGNAL. mStar (194028): 'BN yakin menang majoriti mudah PRN Negeri Sembilan, bentuk kerajaan tanpa parti lain' (BN confident of an easy simple majority in the NS polls, forming government without other parties). This is BN's public self-assessment of its electoral position. Neutral-to-slightly-positive for BN momentum; logged neutral as a self-asserted claim (not an independent forecast or poll).
neutral.rationale: mStar (194028): BN's confidence in a simple majority without coalition partners. This is a BN-momentum messaging datum (PIR-07): BN projects it can win outright. It sits in tension with the structural reality (BN contests 25 of 36; the BN-PN understanding is the mechanism by which BN hopes to consolidate the anti-PH Malay vote). Neutral as a self-claim; watch whether independent polls/forecasts in Day-2 daytime coverage corroborate or contradict it. Note: 'tanpa parti lain' (without other parties) is notable framing given the BN-PN understanding — may signal BN intends to govern alone even where PN assisted.
neutral.source_reliability: MEDIUM-HIGH — mStar (The Star's Malay-language portal); BN-source self-assessment.

neutral.entity: [Rev6Δ][PIR-07] BN-PN electoral understanding — extends beyond NS: 'penentu di Melaka, PRU16' (structural significance escalates)
neutral.trend: NEW — SCOPE EXPANSION. Utusan (194028): 'Persefahaman BN-PN di N. Sembilan penentu di Melaka, PRU16' (the BN-PN understanding in NS is a determinant for Melaka and PRU16). The BN-PN electoral understanding — confirmed operational in rev5 — is now framed as structurally significant beyond NS: the NS arrangement is a template/determinant for Melaka and the next general election (PRU16).
neutral.rationale: Utusan (194028): the BN-PN understanding in NS framed as determinant for Melaka + PRU16. This extends the structural significance of the BN-PN pact from a single-state electoral arrangement to a multi-cycle coalition-shaping mechanism. It is the backdrop against which the PIR-06 Bersatu-exit motion plays out: Bersatu is excluded from the BN-PN understanding that is now being framed as the future of the BN/PN relationship. Neutral (structural context); reinforces the PIR-06 read that Bersatu is being structurally sidelined. PIR-07 + PIR-06 overlap. Watch: whether the Melaka/PRU16 framing draws a Bersatu or PH response.

reinforced_carryover.entity: [Rev6Δ-reinforced][PIR-06 CRITICAL] Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin (Bersatu PRESIDENT) — 'true opposition' + 'new bloc after NS polls' consolidation
reinforced_carryover.trend: REINFORCED (not a new escalation). Muhyiddin was scored 0.80 in rev5 (exit-hint). The 194028 cycle adds two reinforcing statements: (a) 'Muhyiddin dakwa Bersatu kini pembangkang tunggal' / 'Bersatu the only true opposition left following cooperation between BN and PN, says Muhyiddin' (Utusan + The Star) — reframes Bersatu as the sole true opposition post BN-PN pact; (b) 'Bersatu to form new coalition/bloc after Negri polls, says Muhyiddin' (The Star) — the explicit concrete post-exit plan first signalled in rev5. These CONSOLIDATE the rev5 exit-hint (principal-level departure signaling) but do NOT escalate it to a new qualitative tier. Score nudged 0.80→0.81 (+1.25%, well below the 30% threshold). NO direction flip. NO formal exit notice.
reinforced_carryover.rationale: Utusan + The Star (194028): Muhyiddin's 'sole true opposition' framing + explicit 'new bloc after NS polls' plan. Combined with the KINIGUIDE 'shifting alliances' analytical consolidation and the graft-trial surfacing, the rev5 two-sided motion is now the consolidated mainstream frame — but no formal PN-MT removal notice and no formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued. The PIR-06 CRITICAL flag carries forward; this revision does NOT raise a NEW critical shift (the escalation was rev4→rev5; rev5→rev6 is consolidation, not further escalation).
reinforced_carryover.source_reliability: HIGH — Utusan + The Star (gnews) + Malaysiakini KINIGUIDE; multi-source.

sentiment_distribution.note: Revision-6 delta merge. The rev6 delta (0 pos / 5 neu / 2 neg NEW + 1 reinforced carryover) is a CONSOLIDATION cycle, not an escalation cycle. The dominant development is the ANALYTICAL CONSOLIDATION of the rev5 PIR-06 two-sided motion into the mainstream campaign master-narrative (KINIGUIDE 'shifting alliances' Top Story) + Muhyiddin's 'true opposition'/'new bloc' reinforcing statements + the BN-PN understanding's scope expansion to Melaka/PRU16. NO formal PN-MT removal notice and NO formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued (confirmed across THREE consecutive overnight passes: 182751, 194028, 205755). The PIR-09 axis adds a NEW candidate-credibility/legal datum (Tamim Dahri surrenders to police, intends to campaign for an independent) and a corroborating legal datum on the PIR-06 principal (Muhyiddin graft-trial surfacing). The PIR-07 axis adds MCA-leadership (Wee 'not a merger') and PM (Anwar manages DAP tension) management statements + a BN self-confidence signal. No entity exceeds the 30% numeric shift threshold (largest tracked: Muhyiddin +1.25%). No direction flips. NO NEW critical qualitative shift — the PIR-06 CRITICAL flag carries forward from rev5 (the escalation was rev4→rev5); rev5→rev6 is consolidation, not further escalation.

pir_weighted_entity_scores.description: Entities tagged with director-approved PIR priority tags (PIR-06/09/07) receive a priority_weight multiplier (1.3–2.0) applied to the base score. weighted_score = score × priority_weight. Negative-sentiment entities with high weighted scores are the highest-priority watch items. List shows the top watch items carrying the rev6 delta + the highest-priority carryover.

critical_qualitative_shift.prior_state_rev5: TWO-SIDED/THREE-SIDED principal-level coalition-restructuring motion. Bersatu PRESIDENT Muhyiddin publicly hints at exit; Annuar Musa corroborates; PAS openly sidelines Bersatu; Hadi blame-shifts; mainstream 'imminent?' framing. CRITICAL flag raised (rev4→rev5 escalation).
critical_qualitative_shift.new_state_rev6: CONSOLIDATED (not further escalated). The rev5 two-sided/three-sided motion is now the consolidated mainstream campaign master-narrative: (a) Malaysiakini KINIGUIDE 'Shifting alliances as battle royale for Negeri Sembilan begins' (Top Story analytical wrap); (b) Muhyiddin's 'sole true opposition' + explicit 'new bloc after NS polls' reinforcing statements; (c) BN-PN understanding scope-expansion to Melaka/PRU16 (the structural mechanism excluding Bersatu); (d) Muhyiddin graft-trial surfacing (corroborating negative on the principal). NO formal PN-MT removal notice and NO formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued — confirmed across THREE consecutive overnight passes (182751, 194028, 205755), each returning formal_removal_notice_detected=false and 0 new PIR-06 content in the 205755 pass.
critical_qualitative_shift.classification: PIR-06 CRITICAL FLAG CARRIES FORWARD (NO NEW critical shift in rev6). The escalation was rev3 (precursor) → rev4 (senior-figure advocacy) → rev5 (two-sided principal-level exit signaling). rev5→rev6 is CONSOLIDATION/CONFIRMATION, not a further qualitative escalation. Per director directive ('Flag any CRITICAL sentiment shift related to PIR-06'), the flag REMAINS ACTIVE (the motion is CRITICAL and ongoing) but this revision records NO NEW critical shift — the trajectory is unchanged and the trigger has not fired.
critical_qualitative_shift.director_directive_met: YES — the PIR-06 CRITICAL flag is carried forward and the rev5→rev6 transition is explicitly classified as consolidation (not a new critical shift) for precision. The director directive to 'flag any CRITICAL sentiment shift related to PIR-06' is satisfied: the CRITICAL state is flagged as ongoing, and the absence of a new shift in rev6 is itself a material finding (the motion is holding at the rev5 escalated tier without crossing into formal notice).
critical_qualitative_shift.trigger_proximity: UNCHANGED vs rev5. Trigger #2 (coalition restructuring / realignment) remains reachable via EITHER path (PN-MT formal removal OR Bersatu formal exit) but has NOT fired. Per collection_metadata trajectory assessment: most likely near-term outcome remains Bersatu voluntary realignment/exiting AFTER the Aug 1 poll rather than a formal PN-MT expulsion beforehand — but a reactive PN-MT removal notice remains possible if Bersatu candidates actively damage PN partners in the 8 Tier-4 seats (N.04/05/13/14/23/25/31/34). verdict: CAMPAIGN DAY 1 DEEP-OVERNIGHT CONSOLIDATION (05:04 MYT 19 Jul) — the PIR-06 CRITICAL flag is CARRIED FORWARD; this revision records NO NEW critical shift (the escalation was rev4→rev5; rev5→rev6 is consolidation, not further escalation). The rev5 two-sided/three-sided principal-level coalition-restructuring motion is now CONSOLIDATED as the mainstream campaign master-narrative: Malaysiakini's KINIGUIDE 'Shifting alliances as battle royale for Negeri Sembilan begins' (Top Story analytical wrap) frames the entire election under the Bersatu-exit / shifting-alliances story; Bersatu PRESIDENT Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin reinforces with 'sole true opposition' (Utusan + The Star) and an explicit 'new bloc after the Negri polls' plan (The Star); the BN-PN electoral understanding is framed as structurally significant beyond NS ('penentu di Melaka, PRU16', Utusan) — the mechanism excluding Bersatu; and the Muhyiddin graft-trial (Jana Wibawa RM24.4m + Bersatu RM1m) resurfaces as a corroborating credibility datum on the principal. The motion remains THREE-SIDED: PN-MT-may-remove-Bersatu (Kiandee, carryover) + Bersatu-may-exit-PN (Muhyiddin/Annuar Musa, reinforced) + PAS-sidelining-Bersatu (Hadi, carryover). NO formal PN Supreme Council (Majlis Tertinggi) removal notice and NO formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued — confirmed across THREE consecutive overnight passes (182751, 194028, 205755), each returning formal_removal_notice_detected=false. CAVEAT: the Annuar Musa 'part-ways' speaker-label is ambiguous (known Bersatu Sec-Gen is Hamzah Zainudin; the prominent Annuar Musa is the BN/UMNO veteran) — retained with reduced confidence; this does not change the two-sided assessment (Muhyiddin's exit-hint is independently corroborated). The PIR-09 axis adds a NEW candidate-credibility/legal datum (activist Tamim Dahri surrenders to police in Port Dickson, intends to campaign for an unnamed NS independent). The PIR-07 axis adds MCA-leadership (Wee 'BN-PN not a merger') and PM (Anwar asks DAP to postpone decision) management of the intra-unity DAP-vs-BN/MCA tension downward, plus a BN self-confidence signal ('majoriti mudah') and the BN-PN scope-expansion to Melaka/PRU16. The PIR-06 CRITICAL flag carries forward per director directive — the motion is CRITICAL and ongoing, but rev5→rev6 is consolidation, not a new escalation. Trigger #2 (coalition restructuring) is UNCHANGED — reachable via EITHER path (PN-MT removal OR Bersatu exit) but NOT fired. Per trajectory assessment, the most likely near-term outcome remains Bersatu voluntarily realigning/exiting AFTER the Aug 1 poll; a reactive PN-MT removal notice remains possible. No Bersatu candidate withdrawals in the 8 Tier-4 seats (N.04/05/13/14/23/25/31/34). PH-favorable tilt holds and consolidates via the master-narrative consolidation of the opposition fracture.
